user_message: This just came in from Steven Wilson at Tailspin Toys. Can you build the analysis?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 10:55 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Tailspin Toys

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 12:15 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 11:26 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Tailspin Toys

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Michelle (Tailspin Toys)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:09:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:13:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:21:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:23:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:26:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
